mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of using multiple catch clauses in Java?

options.text: To handle different types of exceptions separately
To handle the same type of exception multiple times
To handle exceptions in a specific order
To handle exceptions in a random order

mcqs.explanation: Multiple catch clauses allow a single try block to handle different types of exceptions separately, improving code readability and maintainability.

mcqs.question: What happens if an exception is not caught by any catch block in the current method?

options.text: The program terminates
The exception is propagated to the calling method
The exception is ignored
The program continues executing

mcqs.explanation: If an exception is not caught by any catch block in the current method, it will be propagated to the calling method, allowing it to handle the exception or propagate it further up the call stack.

mcqs.question: Why is the order of catch blocks important in Java?

options.text: To ensure that the most specific exceptions are caught first
To ensure that the least specific exceptions are caught first
To ensure that the exceptions are caught in a specific order
To ensure that the exceptions are caught in a random order

mcqs.explanation: The order of catch blocks is crucial because the more specific exceptions should be caught first, preventing them from being caught by a more general catch block.

mcqs.question: Can a try block be followed by multiple catch blocks?

options.text: Yes
No
It depends on the code in the try block
It depends on the types of exceptions that may be thrown

mcqs.explanation: A try block can be followed by multiple catch blocks, allowing each catch block to handle a specific type of exception.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the finally block in Java?

options.text: To handle exceptions that are not caught by any catch block
To execute code that should always be executed, regardless of whether an exception is thrown
To execute code that should only be executed if an exception is thrown
To execute code that should only be executed if no exception is thrown

mcqs.explanation: The finally block is used to execute code that should always be executed, regardless of whether an exception is thrown, making it useful for cleaning up resources or performing other necessary tasks.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions in Java?

options.text: Checked exceptions are thrown at runtime, while unchecked exceptions are thrown at compile-time
Checked exceptions are thrown at compile-time, while unchecked exceptions are thrown at runtime
Checked exceptions are handled by the JVM, while unchecked exceptions are handled by the programmer
Checked exceptions are handled by the programmer, while unchecked exceptions are handled by the JVM

mcqs.explanation: Checked exceptions are thrown at compile-time, while unchecked exceptions are thrown at runtime, making it essential to handle checked exceptions explicitly in the code.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the multi-catch syntax in Java?

options.text: To catch multiple exceptions with a single catch block
To catch a single exception with multiple catch blocks
To handle exceptions in a specific order
To handle exceptions in a random order

mcqs.explanation: The multi-catch syntax allows catching multiple exceptions with a single catch block, making the code more concise and easier to read.
